title: shorea assamica subsp. globifera
intro: Shorea assamica subsp. globifera, also known as the Raj Mahua or Rajmohua, is a plant species belonging to the family Dipterocarpaceae, native to the northeastern regions of India and parts of Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Shorea assamica subsp. globifera include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit immunomodulatory effects, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant include extracts, oils, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions and can be obtained through steam distillation or solvent extraction.
